title: Electronics manufacturer Murata to explore Xona satellite timing service for telecom, data centers
summary: Murata is evaluating Xona's satellite timing service to enhance backup capabilities for GPS-dependent systems in telecom and data centers. Xona is building a low Earth orbit network to support critical infrastructure.

title: Europe is rearming together — except in space
summary: Europe is strengthening its security measures in response to Russian aggression and concerns about U.S. commitment, but space defense remains an exception. The continent is focusing on collective defense while lagging in space militarization.

title: Muon Space unveils Starship-class satellite platform for orbital data centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a Starship-class satellite platform tailored for orbital data centers, with plans for an initial launch in 2028 after securing customers.

title: Blue Origin seeks to resume New Glenn launches by year’s end
summary: Blue Origin aims to resume New Glenn launches by the end of the year after assessing launch pad damage as less severe than initially feared. The company's CEO indicated the rocket could return to flight within months.

title: NRO nominee says commercial space, AI are reshaping spy satellite agency
summary: Roger Mason emphasized the need for clear government demand signals as commercial space and AI transform the space intelligence market during a confirmation hearing. The nominee highlighted how private investment is reshaping the National Reconnaissance Office (NRO).

title: Global SSA Market to Reach $61B as Governments Prioritize Space Security, Resilience, and Orbital Safety
summary: The global Space Situational Awareness (SSA) market is projected to reach $61 billion as governments focus on space security, resilience, and orbital safety. Novaspace's latest SSA report highlights a pivotal decade ahead for the industry.

title: The importance of determining an equilibrium state for space traffic management
summary: The rapid expansion of human and robotic activity in low Earth orbit highlights the critical need for effective space traffic management (STM) to maintain an equilibrium state. Implementing STM is essential to ensure safety and sustainability in increasingly crowded orbital environments.

title: UK explores Vast space station mission for astronaut with physical disability
summary: The UK government is considering sending astronaut John McFall to Vast's Haven-1 space station, potentially making him the first person with a physical disability to live in orbit. This mission highlights efforts to promote inclusivity in space exploration.

title: Impulse Space raises $500 million
summary: Impulse Space has secured $500 million in funding to scale up production of orbital transfer vehicles and other spacecraft. The capital will help meet increasing commercial and government demand for its services.

title: Voyager to acquire lunar lander developer Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Technologies is set to acquire Astrobotic, a company focused on lunar landers and reusable suborbital vehicles, in a deal valued up to $300 million. This acquisition aims to enhance Voyager's capabilities in lunar exploration and transportation.

title: Blue Origin says New Glenn rocket will launch again 'before the end of the year' after explosion
summary: Blue Origin plans to relaunch its New Glenn rocket before the end of 2026 after a previous explosion, aiming to complete repairs and return to the launchpad. CEO Dave Limp acknowledged the ambitious timeline.

title: Sun erupts with 3 colossal solar flares in less than 24 hours, boosting chances for northern lights
summary: The Sun emitted three massive solar flares within 24 hours, causing radio blackouts on Earth and potentially enhancing northern lights displays as solar storms approach. These events increase the likelihood of geomagnetic activity impacting our planet.

title: NASA's MAVEN Mars orbiter is officially dead after months of radio silence
summary: NASA's MAVEN Mars orbiter has been declared dead after months of lost contact due to a Red Planet anomaly. The mission, which studied Mars' atmosphere, has officially ended.

title: Spain's total solar eclipse 2026 comes with a catch — here's how to avoid ruining your view
summary: Spain's total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, occurs near sunset, requiring careful planning to avoid obstructed views. Eclipse chasers must research optimal viewing locations to ensure a clear experience.

title: Happy Pride Month! Remembering Sally Ride's historic legacy | Space photo of the day for June 3, 2026
summary: Sally Ride made history as the first American woman in space in June 1983 and was later revealed to be an LGBTQ trailblazer. The news highlights her legacy during Pride Month.

title: Should we store Mars samples on the moon to keep alien germs away from Earth?
summary: Scientists propose storing Mars samples on the moon to prevent potential alien germs from reaching Earth. A moon base could serve as a biological defense against harmful extraterrestrial samples.

title: New 'Stargate' show cancelled at Amazon MGM
summary: The new 'Stargate' show has been cancelled by Amazon MGM Studios, despite initial plans to continue the franchise.

title: Astrophotographer captures breathtaking view of 548 galaxies from a balcony
summary: An astrophotographer captured a stunning image of 548 galaxies, including the gravitationally bound 'Leo Triplet,' using over 60 hours of light data from a balcony. This achievement highlights the accessibility of deep-space photography with amateur equipment.

title: This star system creates a rare triple eclipse. Here's what that would look like
summary: A rare triple star system where all three stars eclipse each other from our viewpoint is being highlighted as one of the most studied stellar trios. This phenomenon provides unique insights into stellar dynamics and eclipsing binary systems.

title: 'The movie has tremendous heart': We sat down with the director of 'Masters of the Universe' to learn how he found 'the power' for the new He-Man
summary: The director of the new He-Man movie 'Masters of the Universe' discussed finding inspiration for the film, aiming to please his younger self who loved the character. The movie is praised for its emotional depth and connection to the original franchise.

title: Journey to the Center of the Virgo Cluster
summary: NASA/ESA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88) as it journeys through the Virgo Cluster. The galaxy is located about 63 million light-years away in the constellation Coma Berenices.

title: NASA Drains 66-Million-Gallon Reservoir to Upgrade Critical Water System
summary: NASA drained a 66-million-gallon reservoir at Stennis Space Center to upgrade its High Pressure Industrial Water Facility, which supports rocket engine tests. The project involved pumping out 40 million gallons to reach the lowest water level since the 1960s.

title: NASA Says Farewell to MAVEN Mars Mission, Hosts Media Call Today
summary: NASA's MAVEN mission, the first dedicated to studying Mars' atmosphere, has concluded after over 11 years in orbit. The spacecraft lost contact with Earth in December 2023 after exceeding its primary mission duration by a decade.

title: Typhoon Jangmi
summary: The news discusses Typhoon Jangmi, which is expected to bring heavy rainfall across southern Japan. This meteorological event could impact space industry operations in the region.

title: NASA Space Roboticist Challenge
summary: NASA's Fly Foundational Robots (FFR) mission will launch a seven-degree-of-freedom robotic arm to low Earth orbit, with access granted to select U.S. researchers for experiments.

title: Look Up!
summary: ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot and NASA astronaut Jack Hathaway monitored the SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft docking to the ISS from the cupola. The event occurred during Expedition 74 at an altitude of 259 miles above Earth.

title: NASA Testing Wastewater Treatment Facility for Future Moon Base
summary: NASA has sent a mobile wastewater treatment system to the University of North Dakota for testing under lunar-like conditions. The technology aims to support future long-duration missions on the Moon and Mars.

title: Be a Clump Scout and Help Reveal Secrets of Stellar Nurseries
summary: A citizen science project invites the public to help identify star-forming clumps in galaxy images and train machines to automate the process. This initiative aims to advance our understanding of stellar nurseries.

title: Spacewalking With Scott Wray, Artemis EVA Training Lead
summary: Scott Wray shares his lifelong passion for spacewalks, beginning with imaginative play as a child. He now serves as the Artemis EVA Training Lead, overseeing astronaut preparation for lunar missions.

title: Fire’s Footprint on Santa Rosa Island
summary: A wildland fire burned grassland, coastal sage scrub, and chaparral across one-third of Santa Rosa Island, the second largest of the Channel Islands. The fire's impact on the island's ecosystem is significant.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has started inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions planned from five different launch providers.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into Webb's 'Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances our understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket experienced a significant anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36, resulting in an explosion. The incident marks a major setback for the company's efforts to develop its heavy-lift launch vehicle.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to establish foundational infrastructure. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar projects to support this goal.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted the first flight of Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four Falcon 9 Starlink missions and Amazon's Leo satellite launches as the highlights.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been engaged in vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance activities throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research in low Earth orbit.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: Shenzhou 23 successfully launched a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 2022. This mission marks another step in China's ongoing space station operations.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from SpaceX's facilities. The mission demonstrated significant improvements and impressed during its debut flight.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news item. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates key milestones and contributions in space exploration.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance for the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh ideas from these researchers.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space activities.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute system underwent a critical test known as 'the great parachute bake-out' to ensure its reliability in extreme conditions. The bake-out process likely involved heating the parachute to simulate re-entry temperatures.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space introduced Condor-Ultra, a new satellite bus three times larger than its predecessor, targeting the emerging orbital data center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to return to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement strategy. The move follows industry feedback influencing the agency's approach.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of the year after assessing damage to its launch infrastructure. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight timeline on social media.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO) within the Pentagon, which are tasked with accelerating space acquisition. This move aims to streamline defense space programs but could disrupt ongoing efforts to modernize military space capabilities.

title: Northrop Grumman Taps Apex for Golden Dome SBI Collab
summary: Northrop Grumman has partnered with Apex to collaborate on the Golden Dome SBI program, aiming to deliver on-orbit missile defense capabilities by 2027.

title: Voyager Will Acquire Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Space Holdings will acquire Astrobotic to expand its lunar infrastructure capabilities. The move aims to support NASA's Artemis program by providing end-to-end lunar surface solutions.

title: Impulse Space Closes $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has closed a $500 million Series D funding round to continue its current trajectory. The company's President and COO emphasized that the funds will be used to maintain their existing business direction.

title: Bellatrix Aerospace Tapped to Build Korean VLEO Demo Sat
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace is collaborating with Korean optical payload manufacturer TelePIX to build a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) demonstration satellite, planned for launch no earlier than 2028.

title: Opinion: Commercial Space Is Generating Signals Decision-Makers Cannot Hear
summary: Commercial space activities are producing unprecedented operational signals, but decision-makers struggle to process them due to a broken mechanism. The sector's rapid growth is outpacing the ability to effectively communicate critical data.

title: Nuggets in the SpaceX S-1 and Our Thoughts on the Business
summary: SpaceX's IPO prospectus has been released, highlighting key business details. The document provides insights into the company's financial and operational strategies.

title: Impulse Space Raises Another Massive Funding Round in $500M Series D
summary: Impulse Space has raised $500 million in a Series D funding round led by 137 Ventures and Banner VC. The company is focused on advancing space transportation and logistics technologies.

title: Iceye U.S. Appoints ASRC Federal Exec Ann Stevens as CEO
summary: Iceye U.S. has appointed Ann Stevens as CEO of its subsidiary, with Eric Jensen transitioning to COO. Stevens joins from ASRC Federal.

title: MDA Space to Supply Antennas to BAE Systems for Epoch 2 Missile Warning Constellation
summary: MDA Space has been selected by BAE Systems to supply antennas for the Epoch 2 missile warning constellation for the U.S. Space Force. The subcontract involves MDA Space designing and delivering the antennas as part of the constellation.

title: Juniper Research Expects Massive Spike in Direct-to-Cell Subscribers
summary: Juniper Research predicts a significant increase in direct-to-cell (D2C) subscribers, with over 130 million active monthly users expected by 2031. The forecast is detailed in their report 'Direct to Cell Market 2026 – 2031.'

title: Are Orbital Data Centers the Next Frontier of AI Infrastructure?
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers to support AI infrastructure, with competition among hyperscalers and cloud providers driving innovation in space-based computing.

title: Sighting the Dark Fleet: Hyperspectral’s Role in Securing Safer Seas
summary: Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation to U.S. and Israeli strikes, escalating a regional war. Hyperspectral technology is highlighted for its role in securing maritime safety during the crisis.

title: Orbital Data Centers: The Hype is There. Will the Reality Match it?
summary: The article discusses the hype surrounding orbital data centers in the satellite industry and questions whether the reality will live up to expectations. It highlights the industry's tendency to chase the next 'big thing' in space.

title: From Capacity to Capability: The New Playbook for Asia-Pacific Operators
summary: Regional satellite operators in the Asia-Pacific are shifting from traditional capacity-based models to capability-driven strategies, focusing on orchestration and integration amid industry consolidation and megaconstellation growth.

title: How Can We Respond to the Skills Gap in Space?
summary: A 2023 Space Sector Skills Survey by the Space Skills Alliance reveals that 52% of U.K. space organizations face skills gaps in their workforce. The article discusses potential responses to this growing challenge.

title: The Earth Observation Market and Regulation: Instruments for Growth
summary: The Earth Observation (EO) market is highlighted as a strategic tool for Europe's defense and sustainability goals, including monitoring environmental issues like droughts and forest fires.
